the reason that elon musk can't get out of the deal over the bots thing is not that he “waived due diligence.” it's that he SIGNED A BINDING AGREEMENT TO BUY TWITTER, and that agreement does not have any outs for “i think there are too many bots.”

NEW: Elon Musk's deal to buy Twitter is in serious jeopardy, three people familiar with the discussions say. Musk was unswayed by Twitter's data “firehose,” his camp has stopped discussions with at least one likely backer, drastic action could be coming https://www.washingtonpost.com/ ...

To be clear, this is an absurd attempt to move the goalposts, without a single shred of evidence that Twitter is doing anything wrong, that Elon just successfully laundered through the Washington Post
i know this goes without saying but none of these things are things https://www.washingtonpost.com/ ... https://twitter.com/...

I find this entire Twitter-Musk situation to be preposterous considering the unsealed emails and reports regarding Facebook's inflated potential reach and their alleged covering up of fake, duplicate and spam accounts. Remarkable the SEC hasn't stepped in. https://www.wsj.com/...

Here's how this is gonna go: - Elon asks for “more” info - Twitter gives more info - Elon asks for even “more” - Twitter says “we've already given you everything” - Elon uses this as evidence of “noncompliance” and seeks to renegotiate deal You can set your watch to it
at no point in this article is the reader clearly introduced to the concept that the sudden concern about bots is complete bullshit https://www.washingtonpost.com/ ...
Good time to re-up this. https://twitter.com/...
a merger agreement — in US public-company M&A usage — is not an agreement to look into the company, evaluate its business and decide whether you want to buy it. it's an agreement to buy it!

It's Musk that's in peril, not the deal. He's not dealing with the SEC now, he's dealing with a board of finance types who have an unlimited legal budget, famously don't care about Twitter, and only fear being sued themselves if they don't get $54.20 https://twitter.com/...
This new Twitter app layout is so Facebook-y right as Facebook is trying to be more TikTok-y. Also I hate it

bots discussion that has dominated discourse is the tail wagging the dog (the agenda of which has been set by the tweeter-in-chief) if he just like, doesnt give them the money, he can get sued for specific performance https://twitter.com/...
Fine by us. We don't want another unaccountable billionaire running a massive social media platform. #StopTheDeal https://twitter.com/...

Um, this is sourced entirely from the Musk side and taking its word on bots as if it were anything but a feint. But he signed a binding contract and no hand waving negotiation tricks is going to change that: Elon Musk's deal to buy Twitter is in peril https://www.washingtonpost.com/ ...
“due diligence,” in US public-company M&A usage, is a thing that you do BEFORE YOU SIGN THE MERGER AGREEMENT
you do due diligence to decide if you want to buy the company. then, having decided, you sign an agreement to buy the company. at this point due diligence is over because you have made your decision.
Alt headline: Musk finds his excuse to not buy Twitter https://t.co/pgUTRpvddY
Twitter needs to play this as if the deal is still on. Then sue the crap out of him.
I miss the days when Instagram was Instagram, Twitter was Twitter, and Facebook was Facebook, and not them trying to be like the other. Look at TikTok, over there being an individual and thriving!
now: it is true that musk seems to have declined to do much or any nonpublic due diligence. but that is not relevant to anything now.
Gosh. Never saw this coming https://www.washingtonpost.com/ ...

this is significant in that Zuckerberg has kind of gone back and forth on it for some time. For a while you didn't need a Facebook account to log in. Then Zuckerberg wanted it to be a requirement. Now, don't need one again, sort of. https://twitter.com/...

Mark Zuckerberg just announced that later this summer, we'll begin rolling out a new way to log into your VR headset — and it doesn't require a @Facebook account. Here's what you need to know: https://about.fb.com/... https://twitter.com/...
hi twitter it's meta from facebook here's mark on facebook from meta to tell you that you don't need a facebook account to set up your meta account https://twitter.com/...
Quest Pro was the only name that made sense. Also Zuckerberg discussed it as Quest Pro with me last year: https://www.cnet.com/... https://twitter.com/...

It's an improvement, but deeply frustrating that Meta doesn't seem to understand/care that the forced socialness is part of what was annoying about the Facebook requirements. Just let me use hardware without having a searchable profile! https://www.theverge.com/...
Looks like Facebook/Meta is making a concerted effort to center its technical and product announcements around Mark Zuckerberg. https://twitter.com/...

Meta is dropping mandatory Facebook logins from its VR headset platform. In its place will be the Meta account, a new identity system for VR and the metaverse. Here's @jank0 with the details. https://www.protocol.com/... https://twitter.com/...

AFAICT, Horizon Workroom says it won't collect VR data for advertising purposes. The rest of the Meta regime seems to lack such promises. So if your VR data otherwise flows back to FB data mining, ads, and vice-versa, what's the benefit of having a unique account? https://twitter.com/...
Wow Facebook is actually removing the Facebook account requirement, and making “Meta Accounts” starting in August. Catch me surprised. https://twitter.com/...
You asked, I listened. I will stop murdering people with knives and instead stab them with forks https://twitter.com/...

Best news ever! Starting next month you do not need a Facebook account anymore to use a Quest 2 and also future VR headsets. Now the question arises what's the difference between a Facebook and a Meta account? https://twitter.com/...
Meta Quest headsets will stop requiring a Facebook account... they'll require a Meta one instead. ...I guess that's a slightly useful distinction? https://www.theverge.com/...

There is literally nothing FB/Meta could say that would convince me this change is anything but cosmetic, and behind the scenes they will link your Facebook/IG/VR identity for the purpose of ad targeting and other experience customization. https://twitter.com/...
Excited for Meta accounts! https://www.protocol.com/... https://twitter.com/...
seems practical, though ymmv on the difference between a Facebook account and a “Meta account” a rose by any other name etc etc
Hard to express how unprecedented this move is, and what a big commitment it implies for the company and its products... https://techcrunch.com/...

Conversely, it's annoying to me because the Quest is quite open in other ways, i.e. allowing sideloading. It feels counterintuitive to have this heavy up-front gate before letting people put (last I checked) basically whatever apps they want on the hardware.

Don't worry, your new Meta account doesn't need to be linked to your Facebook or Instagram presence. It just *has* to be linked to Horizon, which is the company's latest attempt at making an immersive equivalent of Facebook. A totally acceptable trade-off, right? https://twitter.com/...

🙏 Keeping things separate. As a gamer I appreciate this & I know this was a HUGE blocker for many of my friends wanting to try @MetaQuestVR . Love the FB account requirement is no longer mandatory. More work to be done! https://twitter.com/...

I'm still trying to understand how and why Facebook accounts went from optional on Quest in 2019 to required in 2020 and now they're optional again in 2022. Fascinating to me such dramatic shifts in end policy could happen on such a short time scale. https://twitter.com/... https://twitter.com/...

The improvements to me are a) you can set up multiple accounts that share apps without worrying about Facebook's “one identity” rule and b) it seems like you won't brick your headset if you run temporarily afoul of Facebook's gigantic moderation machine.

It's probably good not to require Facebook login for Meta's VR hardware. But users will be able to create multiple accounts, and don't require real names - I wonder how Meta plans to moderate the platform. https://www.protocol.com/...
@MetaNewsroom @facebook Great move. Thank you! This was why I haven't moved to the Quest 2 despite it being otherwise my favorite platform for VR.

It's particularly weird to say a Meta account is “not a social profile” when opening one requires you to immediately create a social profile! You just added an extra word to the profile part of it! That doesn't count!

It's a conscious move to distance Zuckerberg from the scandals that have plagued the company for the last two years. He wants to be seen as an innovator (like Elon Musk) and it's all part of the company strategy: https://www.nytimes.com/...

@WholeMarsBlog @elonmusk Basically, Starlink Maritime uses two Performance dishes. Performance is double the size of a standard Starlink. Two Perf Dishes quadruples performance — so that helps reduce latency, ping drops, or loss of signal at sea because of the wider FOV provided by the two Dishes.

@WholeMarsBlog No, it's dual, high performance terminals, which are important for maintaining the connection in choppy seas & heavy storms. Still obv premium pricing, but way cheaper & faster than alternatives. SpaceX was paying $150k/month for a much worse connection to our ships!

On one hand, ransomware attacks on hospitals have become a darkly regular occurrence. On the other, this is the first time we're seeing a foreign country develop a business model of holding US medical facilities hostage, presumably for illicit programs. https://www.nbcnews.com/...

“Maui stood out to us because of a lack of several key features we commonly see with tooling from RaaS providers, such as an embedded ransom note to provide recovery instructions or automated means of transmitting encryption keys to attackers.”
“Instead, we believe that Maui is manually operated, in which operators will specify which files to encrypt when executing it and then exfiltrate the resulting runtime artifacts.”

While we don't know of any named hospitals as victims, @uuallan tells me that the industry knows of at least about a dozen healthcare facilities that have been hit. https://www.nbcnews.com/... https://twitter.com/...

That MI5 DG was allowed to say this underscores sea-change in UK policy: “The most game-changing challenge we face comes from the Chinese Communist Party. It's covertly applying pressure across the globe. This might feel abstract. But it's real and it's pressing...We need to act”.

Former Australian politician Sam Dastyari gets a shout out in this speech by MI5 Director General Ken McCallum. And this protective security advice (circa 1990) seems to have aged well. Heads of MI5 and FBI gave their first ever joint speech at Thames House in London this afternoon to audience of business & academic leaders, on danger of Chinese espionage. MI5's Ken McCallum warned of “coordinated campaign on a grand scale” & “strategic contest across decades”.

MI5 DG: Chinese espionage uses “subtle, patient approach” & many agents of CCP are unwitting Westerners. Says MI5 has doubled “previously-constrained” effort v China in last three years, and plans to double again. Seven times as many China-related investigations today as in 2018.

“There is plenty you can do to protect yourself. Proportionately. Without making your organisation, your start-up or your university a fortress” Reminded of my warnings about interested “visitors” to UK university quantum computing labs. Ignored 🤷‍♂️ https://www.mi5.gov.uk/...

For the first time, Heads of the FBI and MI5 shared a public platform today. 🇬🇧🇺🇸 MI5 Director General Ken McCallum and @FBI Director Chris Wray came together to send a clear signal on a massive shared challenge: China. See more: https://www.mi5.gov.uk/... https://twitter.com/...

'The most game-changing challenge we face comes from the Chinese Communist Party. It's covertly applying pressure across the globe. This might feel abstract. But it's real & pressing. We need to talk about it. We need to act.' Sobering unity from US, UK. https://www.mi5.gov.uk/...

Buried because of Boris' woes but really significant they've taken this step of issuing a joint warning. Pertinent timing too for Australia with all the ‘reset’ talk about China: https://www.bbc.com/...
“FBI director Christopher Wray said China was the ‘biggest long-term threat to our economic and national security’ and had interfered in politics, including recent elections.” https://www.bbc.com/...

No business is surprised by this warning - everyone has known and experienced this for years now. However, it's real progress for America's government to join with Great Britain's to acknowledge it. It will be epic if they actually do something about it. https://www.wsj.com/...

I welcome the public disclosure by MI5 of what has been long understood in the intelligence community: the sophisticated, comprehensive and systemic foreign interference, espionage and cyber security threat posed by the Chinese Communist Party: https://www.mi5.gov.uk/...

Announcement from the US Office of Government Ethics: Government employees who hold cryptocurrencies are disqualified from working on any crypto-related policy or regulation that could impact the value of their holdings. https://www.oge.gov/... https://twitter.com/...

“Cryptocurrencies and stablecoins do not meet the definition of ‘publicly traded securities’ for purposes of these exemptions. This is true even if individual cryptocurrencies or stablecoins constitute securities for purposes of the Federal or state securities laws.” https://twitter.com/...

🤦 this is the equivalent of “those that are working on internet policy are not allowed to use the internet.” I wrote the White House memo on Bitcoin in 2014. This is shortsighted. https://twitter.com/...
as this thread notes, this does not apply to congress. there are members of congress who have crypto and work on crypto policy. https://twitter.com/...

us office of government ethics legal advisory notice, paraphrased “only us government employees who completely lack any understanding of cryptocurrency and stablecoins or who actively dislike them shall be permitted to work on us crypto policy” https://cointelegraph.com/...
In order to maintain my objectivity as a scholar of banking crises and regulation, I have decided that I need to close my bank account. https://twitter.com/...

This is good, and a step toward preventing regulatory capture on the issue. NB: those offering banal tu quoque rejoinders in the comments will be muted — regulatory capture is bad, even if it's concurrently happening elsewhere. https://twitter.com/...

This is a tough one IMO. I know regulators who are struggling to attract crypto experts due to similar rules. Parity to TradFi rules is important though. I purposely didn't hold crypto when I was writing crypto legislation and that made learning difficult https://www.oge.gov/...

Of note: This does not affect Congress, as lawmaker and Capitol Hill staffers are — for now — allowed to hold and trade cryptocurrencies regardless of the work they do. About that: https://www.businessinsider.com/ ... https://twitter.com/...

NEW: @Apple's #LockdownMode is radical reduction of the threat surface of an iPhone. Cannot overstate how big a change this is for Apple. So important that people at higher digital risk have the option to harden their phones. Some thoughts 1/ https://www.apple.com/... https://twitter.com/...

This is an excellent step forward and something I hope to see echoed in Android (specifically @GrapheneOS) sooner rather than later, extremely useful for people with advanced threat models like political dissidents, journalists, etc. Very cool move by Apple, here! https://twitter.com/...

Unfortunately, *none* would have been spared. Look at the CVEs in the last 5 years. You can be certain, attackers will always find a way. Defenders job is to detect and react. Visibility is key for blue teams. https://twitter.com/...

To be sure, all tech platforms have problems - @apple included (more below) But today @apple is setting an example of what tech platforms can do to positively impact this space & reduce harms https://twitter.com/...

2/ When you notify users that they've been targeted with sophisticated threats, they inevitably ask: ‘How can I make my phone safer?’ We haven't had many great, honest answers that really make an impact. Hardening a consumer handset is really out of reach.

Imagine the number of NSO victims who would have been spared if Apple and Google employed this 5 years ago. Apple, to its credit, has rolled it out now. I asked Google earlier today if it had plans and I got a non-responsive answer. https://twitter.com/...
I probably can't claim credit for any of this, but I'd like to pretend. https://blog.cryptographyengineering.co m/ ... https://twitter.com/...

I've not yet had the chance to play with the forthcoming iPhone “lockdown” mode, but it appears to be a well thought out set of countermeasures to a very specific set of threats. The challenge will be explaining what it does - and doesn't - protect against.

6/ With #LockdownMode @Apple just raised the bar around what's possible. I'm excited to see how it works out for high risk users. There will be a learning curve & unexpected lessons. I'm also hoping we see similar efforts from other companies. Also, the 2x bounty = smart. https://twitter.com/...

Our goal is to provide users with the very best security. For the few targeted by mercenary spyware, Lockdown Mode will help keep them protected. And we're funding efforts at the @FordFoundation to investigate, expose, and prevent these attacks. https://www.apple.com/...

Apple has debuted a ‘Lockdown Mode’ for high-risk users being targeted by spyware used by governments and law enforcement. We congratulate them for providing protection to human rights defenders, heads of state, lawyers, activists, journalists, and more https://www.apple.com/...
Great product-level pushback against NSO Group-style attacks on activists, dissidents, journalists and more. Good for Apple https://www.axios.com/...
Interesting news from Apple. https://www.apple.com/... https://twitter.com/...

5/In #LockdownMode, we see high-impact changes that stomp on specific attack categories that mercenary spyware companies et al. exploit. Does this cover all possible desirable things? No. Not yet (one can hope!) But this is a broad set of welcome protections. https://twitter.com/...

7/ ‘Where did #LockdownMode come from?’ @apple gets a ton of credit! This can't have been easy. They also get credit for listening to researchers & nonprofits... who have called for versions of this for years. Good to see a big company take the chance.
This is awesome. We need to start distinguishing between types of users and their different threat models. Way to go Apple. https://www.apple.com/...
Apple took a gamble, knowing groups like @citizenlab will continue to do this type of independent research holding them to account Other tech companies should now step up and match

.@apple's donation to the Dignity and Justice fund is very positive The @FordFoundation managed fund will help nurture the growing community of researchers & advocacy orgs holding mercenary spyware vendors accountable... https://twitter.com/...

.@apple's newly released #LockdownMode will reduce the attack surface, increase costs for spyware firms, & thus make it much harder for repressive govts to hack high risk users Let's hope that it also helps cut down on real-life harms... https://twitter.com/...
I want threat notifications when *Apple* attacks me. https://twitter.com/...

Meanwhile, @apple's threat notifications have alerted otherwise unwitting victims & has precipitated numerous new & ongoing investigations worldwide by @citizenlab & others They shook a tree and victims have fallen into ours and others' laps https://twitter.com/...

This is a great and fantastic feature. It's definitely not for everyone and you should not be using it, since it degrades iOS performance and features, but it's great from a security standpoint. https://twitter.com/...
Interesting - It's like Apple just gave us all a list of the services and APIs they don't think are truly secure. Not sure that was the intention... but... 😛 https://twitter.com/...
